data.narrative:
IQM Quantum Computers is a pre-IPO stage quantum computing company that has raised €600M+. The valuation universe consists of 9 comparable companies across quantum computing: 3 public entities (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 6 private firms including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion.

A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the quantum computing sector. While 5 same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow stage and valuation-tier filters yielded the minimum 3 datapoints required for a robust median calculation. An all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of quantum hardware maturity, IP positioning, and commercialization timelines across the peer set.

The analysis therefore cannot triangulate to a supported valuation range using quantitative benchmarking methods on the available data. Any valuation conclusion would require supplementary approaches—such as DCF analysis with explicit technology-risk assumptions, detailed revenue pathway modeling, or case-by-case precedent transaction analysis within the peer set—none of which are provided here. The €600M+ in cumulative capital raised establishes a floor for investor expectations but does not independently establish fair value. Key uncertainties center on commercialization timing, hardware scalability, and competitive positioning relative to the named peer companies.
